title: Lit-T3: lit pdf (arXiv + paywalled via headed Playwright on TIB VPN)
description: lit pdf <id>: try arXiv direct download first; on failure or non-arXiv, use playwright-cli headed Chromium pattern from FQHE/scripts/fetch_via_browser.sh — TIB VPN gives APS (PRL/PRB/PRX/RMP), IOP (J. Phys. A, NJP), Springer (CMP), Elsevier (Nucl. Phys. B, Annals of Physics), World Scientific. Cloudflare-pass-through fetch via page.request.get -> base64 body. Save to literature/pdfs/{slug}.pdf, update papers.pdf_path.

title: Lit-T2: lit fetch-cites (S2 + OpenAlex bidirectional)
description: lit fetch-cites <id> [--direction=both|forward|backward] [--source=s2|openalex|both]: pull both forward (cited-by) and backward (references) from Semantic Scholar Graph API and OpenAlex Works API. Dedup by arxiv-id/DOI/title-fuzzy. Insert into citations table with source tag. Auto-create lightweight stub paper rows for newly seen targets (status=stub).

title: Lit-T1: lit add (arXiv + Semantic Scholar metadata fetch)
description: lit add <arxiv_id|doi>: fetch metadata from arXiv API (title, authors, abstract, year) and Semantic Scholar Graph API (paperId, references, citations counts). Insert/update papers row, populate authors via paper_authors. Idempotent on re-run.

title: Lit-H5: First SURVEY.md + references.bib generation; archive old docs
description: Generate literature/SURVEY.md (Bennett.jl/Sturm.jl REPORTING_STANDARD format: [TAG] Author (YEAR) - Title with arXiv/DOI, PDF status, Key idea, Relevance to project, Cites/cited-by) and literature/references.bib from SQLite. Move existing literature/catalog.md, overview.md, review.md to literature/_archive/ with a README noting they are superseded.

title: Lit-H4: Ingest existing PDFs into SQLite
description: For each PDF in literature/pdfs/: extract title/authors/year (from filename hints + first-page text via pypdf), look up arxiv-id/DOI where possible, insert into papers table. Mark each as status=have_pdf, tier from existing catalog.md. Mark seven anchor seeds in seeds table: Garjani-Ardonne 2016 (1608.04927), Poilblanc 2011 (1112.5950), Poilblanc 2012 (1210.5605), Hollands 2022 (2205.15243), Stottmeister 2022 anyon-RG (2201.11562), Shi-Zhang-Senthil 2025 (2505.02893), Nakajima-Mehta-Goldman 2025 (2508.14961).

title: Lit-H3: Dedupe and canonical-rename existing PDFs
description: literature/pdfs/ has ~40 files with inconsistent names (mix of arxiv_id.pdf, Author-20-Title.pdf, Lastname_topic_year.pdf-2025-untitled.pdf). At least 3 visible duplicates (Bonkhoff vs al.-Bosonic_Continuum_Theory; Ardonne-2017 vs al.-pairing). Resolve duplicates, rename surviving PDFs to canonical {arxiv_id_or_doi_slug}.pdf. Record original filenames in DB for provenance.

title: Lit-H2: Tidy literature/ directory
description: Move stray Garjani-Ardonne jpegs (currently untracked at project root literature/md/GarjaniArdonne2017/_page_*.jpeg) into their proper subfolder structure. Move literature/tex/ (rwolfthesis LaTeX source) to literature/md/rwolfthesis/tex/ for consistency.
close_reason: Moved literature/tex/ -> literature/md/rwolfthesis/tex/. Stray Garjani-Ardonne jpegs were already in their subfolder, just untracked; will be ingested via H4.

title: Lit-H1: SQLite schema + scripts/lit.py CLI skeleton
description: Foundation for the literature-crawl effort. Create literature/db/papers.sqlite with tables: papers (arxiv_id, doi, title, year, venue, authors_str, abstract, pdf_path, md_path, status, tier, notes), authors, paper_authors, citations (src_id, dst_id, source: S2|OpenAlex|GS|manual), seeds, crawl_log. Build scripts/lit.py with subcommands stubbed: add, fetch-cites, pdf, md, gscholar, export, gaps. SQLite is the source of truth; references.bib and SURVEY.md are generated views.
close_reason: Schema in literature/db/schema.sql; CLI in scripts/lit.py with init/status/seed working and remaining subcommands stubbed pointing at their owning beads tasks. DB at literature/db/papers.sqlite is gitignored (regeneratable from references.bib + arXiv/S2).

title: Lit-C9: Triage and tier classification
description: After C1-C8 dump everything into the DB, triage every new hit by reading abstract + relevant sections of paper.md (marker output). Assign tier: T1 directly defines a Fock space H = oplus_N H_N for indeterminate-N anyons from a fusion category; T2 dense-chain ancestry; T3 categorical foundations / subfactor; T4 numerics / methods; T5 not relevant. Update papers.tier and papers.notes.

title: Lit-C7: Pre-2013 lineage sweep
description: Map the pre-Poilblanc literature on multi-anyon Hilbert spaces that the existing catalog has not properly captured. Targets: Wilczek 1980s anyon Fock space papers; Frohlich-Marchetti 2-anyon Fock; Read 1990 mobile-anyon ground states; Read-Rezayi quasihole counting; Bonderson thesis (interferometry, Mor-spaces); Kitaev 2006 cond-mat/0506438 §E; Etingof-Nikshych-Ostrik 2005 + 2009 + 2015 Tensor Categories; Drinfeld; Leinaas-Myrheim; Goldin-Menikoff-Sharp; Wen on anyon Hilbert spaces. Manually-seeded; use lit add + lit fetch-cites for each.

title: Lit-C5: Cite chase Shi-Zhang-Senthil 2025 + the 2025 FCI burst
description: 2505.02893 spawned a wave of 2025 follow-ups (Pichler-Vishwanath 2506.08000, Han-Zaletel-Vishwanath 2508.14894, Schleith-Khalaf 2506.11211, Shi-Senthil 2506.02128 + 2512.17996, Kuhlenkamp 2509.02675, Zhang 2506.00110, Seo-Cho 2602.03848, Divic 2410.18175, Kirchner 2507.22115). Most listed in catalog.md as needs-review. Pull metadata + PDFs, fetch citation graph, identify any further hits we havent seen.

title: Lit-C4: Cite chase Stottmeister 2022 (2201.11562) anyon-RG
description: Closest prior art on the OAR continuum-limit side. Forward chase to find any anyon fine-graining / inductive-limit work since. Backward chase Osborne-Stottmeister 2107.13834, 2002.01442, 1901.06124 — likely catches OAR papers we havent yet linked to anyon Hilbert spaces.

title: Lit-C3: Cite chase Hollands 2022 (2205.15243)
description: The most rigorous categorical formalisation (subfactor / Mor(1, X1 tensor ... tensor Xn)). Forward chase: who has built on the subfactor formulation since 2022, especially anyone moving to mobile / variable-N / fine-graining. Backward: chase Jones tunnel / TLJ / 6j-symbol references that may seed missing categorical papers.

title: Lit-C2: Cite chase Poilblanc 2011 + 2012 (1112.5950, 1210.5605)
description: The closest prior art on itinerant non-abelian anyons. Existing catalog notes ~18 GS citations on 1210.5605 with 13 relevant; harmonise with S2/OpenAlex, complete the cited-by graph, backward-chase both papers. Particularly look for follow-ups on charge-anyon fractionalization and SU(2)_k extensions.

title: Lit-C1: Cite chase Garjani-Ardonne 2016 (1608.04927)
description: Forward + backward citation chase for the variable-N anchor. Existing review notes only 4 GS citations (Shen et al. 2020/2021, plus first-author thesis). Confirm by S2 + OpenAlex + INSPIRE; chase any non-trivial ones. Backward: full bibliography, mark missing PDFs as needs_pdf in DB.

title: Lit-T6: lit export (regenerate references.bib + SURVEY.md from DB)
description: lit export bib: write literature/references.bib with stable BibTeX keys (e.g. PoilblancFeiguinTroyerArdonne2012). lit export survey: write literature/SURVEY.md grouped by tier, Bennett.jl/Sturm.jl REPORTING_STANDARD per-paper format. Both files are generated views — never hand-edit. Add lit gaps subcommand: list papers with status!=reviewed, missing PDFs, dangling stubs, or orphan citations.

title: Lit-T5: lit gscholar (headed Playwright, last-resort fallback)
description: lit gscholar <query|cited-by:title>: launch headed Chromium (use ~/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium-1217), search Google Scholar, scrape results. Used only when S2/OpenAlex/INSPIRE return nothing — GS is the only source for some books, theses, and pre-arXiv papers. Manual Cloudflare/captcha solve OK. Rate-limit aggressively (sleep 8-15s between page loads).

title: Lit-T4: lit md (marker conversion via archivum venv)
description: lit md <id>: invoke marker (lives in /home/tobiasosborne/Projects/archivum/.venv/bin/marker_single) to convert pdf_path -> literature/md/{slug}/paper.md. Reuse the existing scripts/process_pdfs.py logic. Update papers.md_path.

title: Lit-S3: Integrate references.bib into tex/main.tex
description: Replace the inline thebibliography block in tex/main.tex with bibtex/biblatex against the generated literature/references.bib. Verify all citations in tex/sections/finegraining.tex resolve. Add citation entries for all newly-discovered tier-1/2 papers from C1-C9 that should appear in the paper.

title: Lit-S2: Gap diagram across feature axes
description: Produce a single table/figure (in SURVEY.md and tex/sections/) showing for each paper which of the following it covers: arbitrary fusion category, variable N, multiplicities N_ab^c > 1, OBC, explicit braiding in H, isometric fine-graining, continuum limit. Make the genuinely-novel cells of our project visible at a glance.

title: Lit-S1: Per-tier write-up in SURVEY.md
description: For each tier-1 paper (and key tier-2/3) write the standardised entry in SURVEY.md including: the EXACT definition of the multi-anyon Hilbert space the paper uses (fixed-N? variable-N? Mor-space? subfactor? FQHE quasihole?), the fusion category support (SU(2)_k? abelian? UMTC? trivial?), whether multiplicities >1 are handled, whether OBC/PBC, and how it relates to our Mor(X_c, X_a1 tensor ... tensor X_aN) construction.

title: Lit-C8: Adjacent-tradition sweep
description: Look for variable-N anyon Hilbert spaces in adjacent traditions: anyonic Lieb-Liniger / Kundu / Tonks-Girardeau (already partly in overview.md but unreviewed); abelian anyon-Hubbard (Keilmann 2011, Bonkhoff 2025 — already in pdfs); MPS/MERA for anyon chains (Singh-Pfeifer-Vidal 2013, Ayeni-Pfeifer-Brennen 2016, Pfeifer 2010); Levin-Wen mobile string-net excitations; Kitaev quantum-double mobile defects; Walker-Wang. Triage which of these actually treat indeterminate-N.

title: Lit-C6: Cite chase Nakajima-Mehta-Goldman 2025 (2508.14961)
description: Dilute anyon thermodynamics from fusion constraints — complementary continuum approach. Forward chase to find any other thermodynamic/dilute-limit work on multi-anyon Hilbert spaces. Backward chase for older dilute-anyon-gas references.
